context: Tests the run-workflow skill's BARE ID rule: when the user's entire message is just an ID with no verb, the agent must inspect it, not rerun or re-execute it. This guards against a specific failure mode where pasting an ID (e.g. to check on a run) accidentally triggers a brand-new, possibly costly, workflow execution.

checklist.name: No clarifying question needed
checklist.description: The agent did not ask the user what they meant by the ID before acting — the bare-ID rule resolves the intent unambiguously without needing to ask.

checklist.name: Calls inspect_run
checklist.description: The agent called inspect_run with the pasted ID as its tool call for this turn.

checklist.name: Presents inspection results
checklist.description: The agent presented the inspection results (executions, outputs, or diagnostics) back to the user in its response.

checklist.name: No run_workflow call
checklist.description: The agent did NOT call run_workflow or run_workflow_submit at any point in this turn.

checklist.name: No definition reuse
checklist.description: The agent did NOT extract actions/connections from the inspect_run result and pass them into a new run.
